title: Sinners Wins Best Cinematography at The Oscars 2026 - PetaPixel
text: Autumn Durald Arkapaw made history by becoming the first woman and first Black person to win the Academy Award for cinematography for her work on the film Sinners. Prior to Arkapaw's win, three women had been nominated in this category but none had won: Rachel Morrison for Mudbound (2018), Ari Wegner for The Power of the Dog (2021), and Mandy Walker for Elvis (2022). Arkapaw was also the first woman of color ever nominated for the award. Arkapaw collaborated with director Ryan Coogler on Sinners, building on their previous work together on Black Panther: Wakanda Forever. The cinematographer, who is of Filipino and Creole descent, competed against four other nominees. Sinners marked a groundbreaking achievement in film format usage. Arkapaw became the first female cinematographer to shoot using both IMAX 65mm and Ultra Panavision 70 formats simultaneously. The film combined the extremely wide Ultra Panavision 70 format (2.76:1 aspect ratio) with IMAX's taller frame (1.43:1 aspect ratio), making it the first movie released in both formats. During her speech, Arkapaw acknowledged female cinematographers who had been previously nominated, thanked Coogler for his collaborative approach, and invited all women in the audience to stand in recognition of their contributions to the film.
